prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: What My Sister Buried
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Court-Ordered Lifetime
Tagline: Love lives here. So does arson.
Theme: A town never forgets. It just redecorates.
Omen: a lake bell ringing without wind
Relationship dynamic: overbearing mother tension
Props: scarf with impossible timing, ceramic owl, snow globe

Cast:
- Tess Cross | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=lawyer | threat_aura=0.34 | space=storage unit described as seasonal materials | secrets=once left town without explaining why; buried one traumatic detail under work for years
- Elias Pike | archetype=decorative_husband | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=hospital administrator | threat_aura=0.53 | space=unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files | secrets=moved money during a family emergency; was in the wrong county on the wrong night
- Evelyn Bennett | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=bartender who knows everybody's business | threat_aura=0.34 | space=marina tackle room described as old family things | secrets=once hid a casserole dish containing evidence; may die first or survive unbelievably
- Daniel Pruitt | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=therapist | threat_aura=0.72 | space=dock office with a desk nobody fully claims | secrets=knows why the old orchard chapel was closed
- Luke Bell | archetype=rugged_scamp | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=Christmas tree farmer | threat_aura=0.36 | space=side room with a space heater and old family records | secrets=had an engagement that ended under suspiciously quiet circumstances
- Tess Bell | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=boutique owner | threat_aura=0.46 | space=old storm shelter from before the divorce | secrets=keeps screenshots like religious texts
- Jenna Dane | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Noah Dane | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Elias Pike is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Tess Cross says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Marigold Falls.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Elias?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Jenna. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Tess. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The scarf with impossible timing is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Daniel."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
